title: Make sync-core.py vendored-copy writes atomic to avoid truncated hook files on interruption
evidence: `with open(dst, "wb") as f: f.write(src_bytes)` writes each vendored hook .py in place. An interrupt (Ctrl-C, crash, disk-full) mid-write leaves a truncated Python file in a plugin's hooks/ directory — a partial enforcement hook that imports/parses incorrectly at the next session in a dev checkout. Contrast with core's own `_hooklib.write_text_atomic` (temp + os.replace) pattern that the rest of the codebase adopted for exactly this reason (reliability-010 precedent in _githooks.py).
impact: Interrupted sync leaves a syntactically broken vendored hook; CI --check catches drift but not before a local session runs the truncated file.
recommendation: Write to a sibling temp file and os.replace() into place, mirroring write_text_atomic (binary mode preserved).
acceptance_criteria: Killing sync-core.py mid-run never leaves a destination file with partial content
